Title: Ensuring idempotency
Content:
In certain payment use cases, it is important to design your system to ensure that duplicated money movement do not happen. Duplicate money movement usually happens in payment flows where merchant can initiate a money movement event with a API request (no end user involvement). This can happen in merchant initiated transactions or when triggering a refunds. In this article, we will talk about how we prevent duplicated requests in different scenarios.

Payment requests scenarios

Payment channel timeout or temporarily unavailable

In our payment requests API, Xendit

Xendit timeout or temporarily unavailable
